description_long: This is a carved stone stele featuring three standing figures in high relief, with the central figure larger and more prominent, likely representing a deity or revered personage. The stele has a pointed, arched top adorned with intricate patterns and motifs, possibly depicting flames or clouds. The two flanking figures are smaller and stand on either side of the central figure, each with distinct postures and attire. The base of the sculpture is rounded and merges into the figures, with additional decorative elements at their feet. The reverse side of the stele is flat and inscribed with several lines of Chinese characters, suggesting a commemorative or religious purpose. The surface is weathered, with a predominantly gray stone coloration and visible signs of age.
description: A carved stone stele with three standing figures in relief, ornate patterns at the top, and inscriptions on the back. The surface is gray and weathered, with intricate details and a pointed arch shape.
description_view_0: The front view shows three detailed standing figures in relief, ornate patterns above, and decorative elements at the base.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the stele's thickness and the high relief of the figures, with minimal surface detail visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flat surface covered with vertical lines of Chinese inscriptions and the pointed arch shape.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the depth of the relief and the smooth, unadorned stone sides.
attribute_subject_matter: three standing figures in relief, likely religious or commemorative
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the object appears isolated against a plain background.
